steps.title: Write standard forms
steps.content:
Move each equation into standard form:
$$
54x-81y=36y+30
$$
$$
54x-117y=30
$$
For the second equation:
$$
ry=\frac{1}{5}-18x
$$
$$
18x+ry=\frac{1}{5}
$$

steps.title: Match parallel slopes
steps.content:
For no solution, the lines must be parallel and distinct. The second equation's $x$-coefficient is 
$$
\frac{1}{3}
$$ of the first equation's $x$-coefficient:
$$
\frac{18}{54}=\frac{1}{3}
$$
Apply the same factor to the $y$-coefficient:
$$
r=\frac{1}{3}(-117)=-39
$$

steps.title: Check the constants
steps.content: The constants are not in the same ratio: $$ \frac{\frac{1}{5}}{30}=\frac{1}{150} $$ Since $$ \frac{1}{150}\ne\frac{1}{3} $$, the lines are distinct, so the system has no solution. The answer is -39.
